channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Ukraine eyes money and tech in return for Middle East drone support
item.description: Ukraine leader previously said advisers were sent to Qatar, UAE, and Saudi Arabia to help thwart Iranian drone attacks.

item.title: Two brothers survive after Israeli troops kill family in occupied West Bank
item.description: Two Palestinian brothers are the only survivors after Israeli troops killed their parents and two siblings in Tammun.

item.title: US Senator Van Hollen: Trump ‘has no idea where this war is going’
item.description: Democratic US Senator Chris Van Hollen argues that US-Israel war on Iran ‘is making America and our allies less safe'.

item.title: Strategic oil release may calm markets but cannot fix Hormuz disruption
item.description: Record oil release steadies markets, but war-driven disruption in the Strait of Hormuz keeps energy supplies uncertain.

item.title: Trump calls for naval coalition to open Strait of Hormuz: Can it work?
item.description: Analysts say US president's solution to secure the strait is fraught with problems.

item.title: Moment Iranian missile hits Tel Aviv
item.description: CCTV footage released by Israeli police shows the moment an Iranian missile struck a street in Tel Aviv.

item.title: Captain of Iran’s women’s team withdraws Australia asylum bid: State media
item.description: Zahra Ghanbari is fifth football player to withdraw her request after seven squad members sought asylum in Australia.

item.title: What do the first two weeks of the war on Iran portend for the future?
item.description: New, critical dynamics are emerging in the region that could reshape it in dramatic ways.

item.title: Keisuke Honda loses US advertising deal over Iran support at World Cup
item.description: Japanese football legend says his opinion prompts US company to cancel advertising campaign before FIFA World Cup.

item.title: ‘We killed dogs’: Israeli troops kill two children, parents in West Bank
item.description: Each person shot in the head in the village of Tammun, while two other children of the deceased couple sustain injuries.
